mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0 percent, labor market tightness supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when UNRATE drops below 4.0 percent (t-test p > 0.05) across three consecutive calendar years.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with stable industrial production indicates housing-led demand expansion without supply bottlenecks, supporting corporate earnings and equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint momentum of HOUST and INDPRO fails to predict SPX 60-day returns with Rank IC greater than 0.05 over any three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where the CPI index exceeds 250, rising federal funds rates compress equity valuations more severely due to heightened real rate sensitivity.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns do not decline more sharply when CPIAUCSL exceeds 250 and FEDFUNDS rises, compared to periods with CPIAUCSL below 250 (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Continuous growth in revolving credit availability lowers borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, directly boosting equity cash flow expectations over the medium term.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns show no positive correlation with RSAFS growth rates over any five-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production drops below 105, manufacturing supply constraints trigger cost-push inflation and margin compression, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO falls below 105 (t-test p > 0.05) across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment coupled with declining housing starts signals a labor market shift toward broader services, indicating a resilient consumer economy that supports equity valuations.
failure_condition: The cross-product of PAYEMS growth and HOUST decline fails to predict 5-day SPX returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: During periods where real GDP growth decelerates below 2 percent annualized, any increase in federal funds rates acts as a liquidity drain that disproportionately harms equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns do not exhibit statistically significant negative skew when GDPC1 growth falls below 2.0 percent and FEDFUNDS rises, relative to high-growth periods.
